description: Scanning electron micrograph showing the surface morphology of Fe-15Mn-8Al alloy after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hours in pure oxygen at 200 torr pressure. The image reveals numerous small oxide protrusions (nodules) densely distributed across the surface, with sizes typically less than one micron as reported in the text. These protrusions represent manganese-rich oxide nodules that grow through the otherwise protective alumina scale. According to the figure caption and paper context, this alloy composition falls within Region III (Fe-(5–10)% Mn–(6–10)% Al), which exhibits the best oxidation resistance by developing continuous protective alumina scales on a totally ferritic substrate. Unlike the larger bulky manganese-rich oxides characteristic of Region II duplex alloys, these small protrusions are not structurally related to the underlying alloy microstructure. The micrograph demonstrates that high manganese concentrations (15% Mn) promote abundant formation of these small nodules even within the optimal composition range, though the alloy maintains protective behavior with relatively low weight gain (0.116 mg cm⁻²). The scale bar indicates 3 μm, confirming the sub-micron scale of these oxidation features.
